Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 16:33 ET
Massive IWM put positioning conflicts with single-name call demand ahead of jobs data
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index puts at 1.61 P/C while single-names show 0.85 call-heavy — classic institutional dispersion hedge into NFP.
  • 2. IWM sees 36,414 April puts worth $127M as small-cap hedging accelerates despite bullish technicals in MRVL/GS.
  • 3. Negative $4.6B SPY gamma amplifies any jobs surprise — VIX 23.9 understates Friday's volatility potential.
NARRATIVE — defensive dispersion setup

The most striking divergence today is the clash between index put demand and single-name call appetite, with ETF P/C at 1.61 versus individual stock P/C at 0.85 — suggesting institutions are hedging portfolios while positioning for stock-specific upside. This dispersion trade setup intensifies ahead of NFP, with implied correlation at just 0.20 indicating maximum single-name opportunity relative to index hedging costs. Small-cap hedging reached extreme levels as someone deployed $127M across 36,414 IWM April 235/238 puts, the largest Russell block in months and conflicting sharply with golden cross technicals in MRVL (+87.0 score) and GS (+69.5). The IWM positioning suggests concern about rate-sensitive small caps into potential NFP strength, while simultaneously GSAT exploded +812% in calls and +446% in puts — pure volatility demand around the satellite communications name with no obvious catalyst. Dealer positioning at negative $4.6B SPY gamma creates maximum amplification risk into Friday's jobs report, with VIX 23.9 likely understating potential moves given the technical setup. Millennium's aggressive Q4 positioning in MSFT (+135%) and AMZN (+398%) faces immediate test as both names show death cross technicals, while their NVDA conviction build (+93%) aligns with the stock's continued outperformance despite broader tech weakness.

Layer 1: Sentiment -27Layer 2: GEX -$4.6BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.61/0.85Layer 9: NFP Friday
IWMBEARISH
Massive put wall
$127M across 36,414 April 235/238 puts represents largest Russell hedge in months, conflicting with golden cross technicals in underlying holdings like MRVL and GS. Positioning suggests rate sensitivity concerns into potential NFP strength.
L3: 36K put blockL5: conflicting technicalsL9: NFP Friday
Apr 235/238P spread, 18K+ each strike
GSATVOL PLAY
Satellite vol explosion
Calls exploded +812% while puts surged +446% with no obvious news catalyst, creating pure volatility demand in satellite communications name. Two-way flow indicates event expectations or technical catalyst positioning.
L3: +812% callsL3: +446% putsL8: no catalyst
Long straddles across strikes
MRVLBULLISH
Golden cross momentum
Technical score of 87.0 with golden cross formation and RSI at 67 creates strongest bullish setup in megacap space. Momentum contradicts broader semiconductor weakness and negative dealer gamma environment.
L5: 87.0 tech scoreL5: golden crossL2: negative gamma
Long calls into momentum

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
